events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles drummer, Ringo Starr, celebrates his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Drummer's Day
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr! From the Cavern Club to global superstardom, his rhythm is iconic. What's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist and astrophysicist, Sir Brian May, celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Sending birthday wishes to the legendary Sir Brian May, who turns 79! His guitar work has inspired generations and crafted some of rock's most unforgettable anthems.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' charismatic frontman, Sir Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Rock'n'Roll Legend
angles.copy: Still moving like Jagger at 83! Happy birthday to the incredible Mick Jagger, whose energy on stage remains unmatched after six decades.

events.title: MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'
events.blurb: On this day in 1981, MTV launched in the US, forever changing music television, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first broadcast video.

angles.name: MTV's Debut
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV hit the airwaves, kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. It truly was a game-changer for music. Remember watching it for the first time?

events.title: Oasis 'Definitely Maybe' anniversary
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, marking a pivotal moment in Britpop.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: It's been 32 years since Oasis unleashed 'Definitely Maybe' on the world. That album defined a generation and still sounds incredible today. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival anniversary
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a defining moment of the 1960s counterculture, took place from August 15-18, 1969.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace and music at Woodstock. An unforgettable event that shaped a generation.

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the passing of the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who died in 1977.

angles.name: Remembering the King
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley today, 49 years since his passing. His music and iconic presence continue to influence artists worldwide. Long live the King.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur shooting
events.blurb: This day marks 30 years since hip-hop icon Tupac Shakur was shot in Las Vegas in 1996.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Thirty years ago today, the music world was rocked by the shooting of Tupac Shakur. His powerful lyrics and influence continue to resonate deeply within hip-hop culture.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the death of guitar legend Jimi Hendrix, who passed away in 1970.

angles.name: Guitar Hero Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix today, 56 years since we lost him. His revolutionary guitar playing changed music forever.

events.title: The Beatles 'Abbey Road' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic 'Abbey Road' album in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring that legendary zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago this month, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover alone is pure legend. What's your top track from it?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Rock Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and fierce attitude have made The Pretenders a true rock institution.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The unmistakable voice of Oasis and Britpop icon, Liam Gallagher, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Mad for It
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! From Oasis anthems to his solo work, he remains a true rock 'n' roll star.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' UK single anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK in 1975.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-one years ago, John Lennon's 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK. A powerful message that still resonates globally today. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music players.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Twenty-five years ago this month, Apple introduced the first iPod. It completely changed how we listen to music and put '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Celebration
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating over six decades of the world's most famous secret agent. What's your favourite Bond film or theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Today would have been the 86th birthday of The Beatles' co-founder and peace activist, John Lennon.

angles.name: Remembering Lennon
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have turned 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to inspire millions around the world.
